title: The Smartest Way to Bet on SpaceX Right Now Is Hiding in Plain Sight
author: Ryan Vanzo
description: Following SpaceX's IPO with $75 billion in fresh capital, the company plans aggressive spending focused primarily on AI infrastructure rather than space solutions. With $26.5 trillion of its $28.5 trillion addressable market tied to AI, SpaceX will need massive power generation capacity for data centers. Small modular reactor (SMR) companies like NuScale Power and Oklo are positioned to benefit from SpaceX's urgent need for nuclear energy solutions, as traditional power sources cannot meet the growing demand.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: SpaceX has raised $75 billion and identified a massive $28.5 trillion addressable market, primarily in AI. The company has clear plans for scaling chip manufacturing and data center infrastructure, positioning it for significant growth.

NuScale is positioned to benefit from SpaceX's urgent need for power generation through its small modular reactor technology. The article explicitly recommends it as a top research pick for investors seeking exposure to SpaceX's spending spree.

Oklo specializes in small modular reactors and is pursuing direct data center sales, making it well-positioned to capture demand from SpaceX's AI infrastructure expansion. The article identifies it as a key beneficiary of SpaceX's power needs.

Tesla is mentioned as a current supplier of Megapacks to SpaceX's xAI business and has a solar arm that could be leveraged long-term, but the article suggests SpaceX will need additional power sources beyond Tesla's current offerings.

title: 1 Reason to Buy NuScale Power Stock, and 2 Reasons to Sell
author: Courtney Carlsen
description: NuScale Power holds a first-mover advantage as the only company with NRC design certification for small modular reactors (SMRs), positioning it well in the growing nuclear energy sector. However, the company faces significant challenges: a previous major project experienced massive cost overruns ($3B to $9.3B) before being cancelled, and its current partnership with ENTRA1 Energy for TVA deployment has drawn investor scrutiny due to ENTRA1's lack of operational history and NuScale's substantial milestone payments ($495M paid, up to $3B potential) without firm commitments. SMRs aren't expected operational until the 2030s, making this a high-risk investment.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While NuScale has a first-mover advantage with NRC certification, the company faces critical challenges: a history of massive project cost overruns, no operational plants yet, unproven business model scalability, questionable partnership with inexperienced ENTRA1 Energy, substantial unfunded milestone payments, and years away from commercialization. These factors outweigh the competitive advantage.

title: Insiders Sell Top Tech Plays: Should Investors Buy, Sell, or Hold?
author: Thomas Hughes
description: Heavy insider selling across tech stocks raises questions about whether it signals a warning or routine profit-taking. Analysis of NuScale Power, Astera Labs, and Dell shows that while insiders are selling, institutional buying, bullish analyst sentiment, and strong business catalysts suggest these stocks may still have upside potential despite the insider activity.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite heavy insider selling in Q1-Q2, institutional ownership is nearly 80% with strong quarterly buying. Bullish long-term outlook as small modular reactor play with Federal support, Trump administration backing for space-based nuclear power by 2028, and TVA power purchase agreement catalyst. Consensus price target indicates ~50% upside.

Aggressive insider selling offset by 300% annual gains and tripled stock price since April 2026, indicating profit-taking. Institutional buying at 3-to-1 pace driven by critical AI data center connectivity products. Strong catalysts include new product launches and inference boom, though stock is 40% above consensus target and may face near-term correction.

Insider selling limited to Silver Lake Partners taking profits from 2026 rally. Institutions (excluding Silver Lake) own 70%+ and accumulated shares over multiple quarters. Strong business fundamentals with AI-optimized servers in high demand. Analyst price target of $700 suggests 70% upside. Fiscal Q2 2027 results expected in September with forecasted 50%+ revenue growth.

Mentioned as key catalyst for Dell and broader AI infrastructure demand. Upcoming results expected to show continued sequential and year-over-year acceleration tied to AI GPU demand, providing impetus for related tech stocks.

title: Why NuScale Power Stock Is Rocketing Higher Today
author: Scott Levine
description: NuScale Power stock surged over 10% following news that Swedish utility Vattenfall selected Rolls-Royce to supply three small modular reactors (SMRs), signaling growing international demand for SMR solutions beyond the U.S. market. However, the article cautions that investors should be aware of significant risks associated with speculative SMR investments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock rallied 10.7% on positive industry news from Vattenfall's SMR selection, demonstrating growing international market opportunities. However, sentiment is tempered by the article's warning about significant investment risks and speculative nature of the company.
Selected by Vattenfall to supply three SMRs, indicating market validation and growth opportunity in the SMR sector. Stock up 3.51% reflecting positive sentiment.

title: This Stock Is Down 40% in 2026. Here's What the Next 3 Years Could Realistically Look Like.
author: Steven Porrello
description: NuScale Power, the first U.S. company to receive NRC approval for small modular reactor (SMR) design, has declined significantly from its all-time highs. The article explores three-year scenarios ranging from slow steady progress on its Romania and TVA projects to more bullish outcomes where commercial partnerships with data centers materialize and validate the technology for broader U.S. deployment.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article presents a balanced view with both bearish and bullish scenarios. While the stock has declined 40% and lacks commercial deployment, the company has first-mover advantage with NRC certification and two major projects underway. Success depends on execution over the next few years, making the outlook uncertain but potentially promising.
